docs.title: DegraTwin
docs.text:
DegraTwin is a Streamlit-based digital twin tool for textile degradation simulation.

The application uses humidity and temperature data to evaluate environmental risk for selected textile materials and simulate degradation under different environmental scenarios.

docs.title: Main features
docs.text:
• Load sensor readings from an external API
  • Evaluate environmental risk for selected textile materials
  • Simulate degradation under different scenarios
  • Display plots and preview tables
  • Export a JSON summary of the simulation

docs.title: Project License

docs.title: Citation
docs.text:
If you use this software, please cite it using the following BibTeX entry:

@software{DegraTwin,
author = {{CERTH-ITI, M4D Group}},
license = {Apache 2.0},

url = {https://github.com/TEXTaiLES/DegraTwin},
version = {0.1.0},
year = {2025}
}

docs.title: Clone the repository
docs.text:
Open a terminal and run:

git clone https://github.com/TEXTaiLES/DegraTwin.git
cd DegraTwin

docs.title: Option 1: Using conda
docs.text:
conda env create -f environment.yml
conda activate degratwin

docs.title: Usage
docs.text: This page explains how to use the DegraTwin application to run a textile degradation simulation.

docs.title: 1. Select simulation parameters
docs.text: Use the sidebar to configure the simulation.

docs.title: Projection years
docs.text: Select how many years the degradation simulation should cover.

docs.title: Harm component weights
docs.text:
Adjust the importance of each degradation factor:

  • Mechanical harm
  • Chemical harm
  • Biological harm
  • Light-related harm

docs.title: Light background option
docs.text: Choose whether light exposure should be ignored or evaluated against a black or white background.

docs.title: Dose limit
docs.text: Set the dose threshold used in the simulation.

docs.title: 2. Load data
docs.text:
Click Load data to import environmental sensor data.

If API data is enabled, the application retrieves humidity and temperature readings from the external data source.

If the API request fails, the application automatically switches to fallback sample data.

docs.title: 3. Run the simulation
docs.text:
Click Run simulation.

The application will:

  • calculate the average temperature
  • evaluate the environmental conditions
  • simulate degradation scenarios
  • generate plots and output data

docs.title: 4. View the results
docs.text:
After the simulation is complete, the application displays:

  • Sensor preview table
  • Relative humidity comparison plot
  • Degradation by scenario plot
  • JSON output summary

docs.title: Typical workflow
docs.text: ```text Select parameters → Load data → Run simulation → Review results → Export JSON
